title: spartina stricta var. townsendii
intro: Spartina stricta var. townsendii, also known as saltwort or Townsend's cordgrass, is a perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Spartina stricta var. townsendii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal issues.
